frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: RL framework where dopamine encodes reward prediction error guiding interval timing

frameworks.name: reward prediction error (RPE)
frameworks.one_line: RPE account of dopamine extended to modulate the rate of timekeeping

frameworks.name: interval timing
frameworks.one_line: behavioral timing of seconds-scale intervals modulated by dopaminergic signals

frameworks.name: biologically plausible plasticity rule
frameworks.one_line: DA-dependent bidirectional update rule whose sign depends on DA-signal timing

core_question: Can a single reward-prediction-error interpretation of dopamine reconcile its conflicting pharmacological and optogenetic effects on interval timing?
key_assumptions: dopamine encodes a reward prediction error
interval timing can be cast as a reinforcement learning problem
striatal timekeeping is shaped by DA-dependent plasticity
manipulations: direction and timing of the dopamine signal
comparison of pharmacological versus optogenetic perturbations
variation of reward rate in interval-timing tasks
key_findings: pharmacology and optogenetics yield opposite-sign effects of DA on interval estimates
a bidirectional DA-dependent update rule can speed up or slow down timekeeping
the direction of the update depends on when the DA signal arrives
model accounts for reward-rate effects and striatal temporal selectivity
interpretations: the RPE role of dopamine naturally extends to modulating timekeeping rate
this unifies reinforcement learning and interval timing theories of DA
